source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: Analytical modelling of a lateral dual gate MESFET for integrated circuit in SiC
paper.abstract: Static analytical model for lateral dual gate MESFET in SiC. First gate is Schottky barrier, second is bipolar junction. SPICE model developed for design-kit. Model accounts for influence of both gates on channel conductivity. Good agreement with experimental measurements.

verdict.summary: Device physics paper with necessary formalism. Equations 1-14 derive from fundamental semiconductor physics (space charge regions, built-in potentials, carrier transport). All formalism enables SPICE model derivation and experimental validation.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Analytical model for dual-gate MESFET in SiC for power electronics
**Level:** 4 (High)
**Rationale:** Semiconductor device modeling requiring physics-based equations. Novel topology combining Schottky and bipolar junctions for dual-gate control.

### Formal Elements
- Definitions: 0 formal definitions
- Theorems: 0
- Proofs: 0
- Equations: 14 (Eq. 1-14)

### Proportionality
Calculated complexity: 14 equations * 3 = 42
Expected range (Level 4): 30-80
Ratio: 0.53x upper bound

**Assessment:** Proportionate - all equations derive from semiconductor physics fundamentals.

### Necessity Test Applied to Each Formal Element

**Eq. 1 - Space charge region W1(x):** NECESSARY - Poisson equation solution for Schottky junction, enables device physics modeling

**Eq. 2 - Space charge region W2(x):** NECESSARY - Bipolar junction SCR, required for dual-gate operation analysis

**Eq. 3 - Built-in potential Vbi2:** NECESSARY - Standard semiconductor junction formula, cannot be expressed without math

**Eq. 4-8 - Channel current derivation:** NECESSARY - Drift current and channel conductivity equations required for analytical SPICE model

**Eq. 9-14 - Blocking conditions and solutions:** NECESSARY - Determine threshold voltages and operating regions

**Verdict:** All 14 equations pass Necessity Test. Each is required for physics-based device modeling that enables SPICE simulation.

### Issues
None identified. Formalism is domain-appropriate for semiconductor device physics.

---

## Citation Analysis

9 references total. Self-citations: 2 (refs [4], [5]) = 22% SCR.
Self-citations are topically relevant (prior work on same MESFET technology and SiC circuits).
No citation ring indicators.

---

## Structural Analysis

Clear structure: Introduction, Device description, Model derivation, Experimental results, Conclusion.
Experimental validation against measured data. Model-measurement comparison in Figs. 3-5.
Limitations acknowledged: floating gate behavior requires further investigation.
Artifacts: Device manufactured by CNM-IMB Barcelona; model equations provided for VHDL-AMS implementation.

---

## Conclusion

High-quality device physics paper with appropriate formalism for Level 4 complexity. All equations derive from first principles and enable practical SPICE modeling. Experimental validation demonstrates model accuracy. Formalism is necessary, not decorative.

paper.title: Packaging approaches for GaN/Si SAW Band Pass Filters with Operating Frequencies above 5 GHz
paper.abstract: Two packaging approaches for SAW band pass filters on GaN/Si operating above 5 GHz. SMD QFN ceramic package and PMMA quasi-wafer level packaging. QFN adds <1dB loss up to 6 GHz. PMMA quasi-WLP adds <0.1dB loss up to 30 GHz. Both tested with CPW transmission lines and SAW-BPFs.

verdict.summary: Applied RF engineering paper with appropriate minimal formalism. S-parameter measurements and EM simulation are standard methodology. Fabricated prototypes with measurement validation. No decorative mathematics.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** RF packaging solutions for >5 GHz SAW filters
**Level:** 3 (Medium)
**Rationale:** Applied RF/microwave engineering. Novel quasi-WLP approach but methodology is empirical characterization.

### Formal Elements
- Definitions: 0
- Theorems: 0
- Proofs: 0
- Equations: 0 (results presented as S-parameter measurements)

### Proportionality
Calculated complexity: 0
Expected range (Level 3): 10-40
Ratio: 0x upper bound

**Assessment:** Under-formalized but appropriate - this is empirical RF characterization, not theoretical derivation.

### Necessity Test
No formal elements requiring testing. Paper presents:
- EM simulations (CST Microwave Studio)
- S-parameter measurements
- Comparative tables

**Verdict:** No formalism theater. Empirical approach appropriate for packaging characterization.

### Issues
None identified.

---

## Citation Analysis

9 references. Self-citations: 2 (refs [5], [6]) = 22% SCR.
Both self-citations are directly relevant (prior SAW-BPF work and prior quasi-WLP demonstration).
No issues identified.

---

## Structural Analysis

Clear structure with two parallel packaging approaches. Each evaluated on CPW-TL test vehicle then applied to actual SAW-BPF. Tables 1-2 summarize key parameters before/after packaging.
Limitations: Implicitly acknowledged through comparison (ceramic QFN limited to 6 GHz vs PMMA to 30 GHz).
Artifacts: Fabricated devices, ceramic fixtures, PMMA caps. Commercial components identified (NTK package, EpiGaN wafers).

---

## Conclusion

Well-executed applied RF engineering paper. Empirical characterization approach is appropriate for packaging evaluation. Two packaging solutions compared with clear metrics. PMMA quasi-WLP demonstrated as viable low-cost solution for mmWave SAW devices.

paper.title: Microfabrication and experimental characterization of an Out-of-Plane MEMS switch
paper.abstract: MEMS switch with vertical movement using surface micromachining. Electro-thermal actuator with photoresist sacrificial layer. CoventorWare and Comsol FEM simulations for electro-thermo-mechanical behavior. Two gap dimensions (500nm, 1um) fabricated and characterized.

verdict.summary: MEMS fabrication paper with FEM simulation. Material properties table (Table 1) and simulation results are standard for the domain. No decorative formalism - results presented graphically from commercial FEM tools.

papers.provenance:

paper.title: Design and fabrication of tubes-guided structure with electrical stimulation module for neural regeneration and in-vivo testing
paper.abstract: Novel biointerface for peripheral nerve regeneration. 3D printed tubular structure with gold electrodes for electrical stimulation. Wi-Fi module with programmable oscillator (SiT1534) for 20Hz stimulation. Protocol for Lewis rat sciatic nerve implantation and functional testing.

verdict.summary: Biomedical engineering device paper. No formal mathematics - describes 3D printing, electrode design, electronic module, and testing protocol. Appropriate for biomedical device development.

paper.title: Design of Over-Temperature Protection for Switched-Capacitor DC-DC Converter Based on Electro-Thermal Simulations
paper.abstract: Over-temperature protection circuit for SC DC-DC converter. Two-step design methodology: electrical-only simulations then electro-thermal simulations. Die temperature distribution analyzed for critical scenarios. Silicon implementation validated with measurements.

verdict.summary: Analog IC design paper with appropriate formalism. Single equation (DeltaT) is necessary for thermal coupling analysis. Design methodology validated with silicon measurements. Circuit schematics are standard representation.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Over-temperature protection for SC DC-DC converter
**Level:** 3 (Medium)
**Rationale:** Analog IC design with electro-thermal co-simulation methodology. Novel contribution is systematic two-step design approach.

### Formal Elements
- Definitions: 0
- Theorems: 0
- Proofs: 0
- Equations: 1 (Eq. 1: DeltaT calculation)

### Proportionality
Calculated complexity: 1 * 3 = 3
Expected range (Level 3): 10-40
Ratio: 0.08x upper bound

**Assessment:** Proportionate - minimal but sufficient formalism.

### Necessity Test Applied

**Eq. 1 - DeltaT = T_HOTSPOT - T_OTPSENSOR:** NECESSARY - defines thermal coupling metric essential for OTP design. Cannot be expressed without this relationship.

**Verdict:** Single equation passes Necessity Test. Paper relies on simulation results and measurements rather than analytical derivations.

### Issues
None identified.

---

## Citation Analysis

13 references. Self-citations: 2 (refs [11], [12]) = 15% SCR.
Self-citations are relevant (prior OTP circuit work and prior electro-thermal simulation methodology).
Normal self-citation ratio.

---

## Structural Analysis

Excellent structure: Problem statement, Design options, Design example with two steps, Measurement validation.
Monte Carlo simulations for process variation (Fig. 7).
Silicon measurements validate design (Fig. 13-14).
Limitations acknowledged: EMI environment affected measurements, low-EMI setup needed.
Artifacts: Silicon implementation in CMOS process. Measurement setup described.

---

## Conclusion

High-quality analog IC design paper. Two-step methodology (electrical then electro-thermal simulation) is the key contribution. Silicon validation demonstrates <1% error on t2OTP. Minimal formalism is appropriate for design methodology paper.

paper.title: An Improved I/O Pin for Serial Communication Interfaces
paper.abstract: Improved I/O pin with push-pull output buffer for serial interfaces. 0.18um CMOS EEPROM process. Operates 1.6V to 5.6V supply range. Supports I2C UFm at 5 Mbps and SPI at 10 Mbps. Topology improvements reduce propagation delay by 2x at low voltages.

verdict.summary: Digital IC design paper with no decorative formalism. Circuit schematics, timing diagrams, and parameter tables are standard IC design representation. No set theory or formal definitions - appropriate for circuit design.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** I/O buffer design for serial communication (I2C UFm, SPI)
**Level:** 2 (Low)
**Rationale:** Digital circuit design with standard topologies. Contribution is optimized topology for wide voltage range and high speed.

### Formal Elements
- Definitions: 0
- Theorems: 0
- Proofs: 0
- Equations: 0
- Tables: 6 (timing parameters - standard IC characterization)

### Proportionality
Calculated complexity: 0
Expected range (Level 2): 0-15
Ratio: Within range

**Assessment:** Appropriate minimal formalism for digital IC design.

### Necessity Test
No formal equations to test. Paper presents:
- Circuit schematics (standard representation)
- HSPICE simulation waveforms
- Timing parameter tables
- Comparative analysis (standard vs improved topology)

**Verdict:** No formalism theater. Standard IC design presentation.

### Issues
None identified.

---

## Citation Analysis

23 references. Self-citations: 0 = 0% SCR.
No self-citations. Good coverage of prior I/O buffer and serial interface work.
No issues identified.

---

## Structural Analysis

Comprehensive structure: System context, Input buffer, Standard output buffer, Improved output buffer, Applications (I2C UFm, SPI).
Extensive simulation characterization across voltage and temperature ranges.
Comparative analysis clearly shows improvement.
Limitations: Not explicitly stated, but silicon validation not presented (simulation only).
Artifacts: Circuit designed in 0.18um CMOS EEPROM process.

---

## Conclusion

Thorough digital IC design paper. Improved topology achieves 2x speed improvement at low voltages. Extensive characterization across operating conditions. No decorative formalism - standard IC design methodology with simulation-based validation.

batch_notes: Volume 22 Issue 2 (2019) analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. **No formalism theater detected.** All 6 papers show domain-appropriate formalism: semiconductor device physics (paper621), RF packaging (paper622), MEMS fabrication (paper623), biomedical device (paper624), analog IC design (paper625), digital IC design (paper626). The MESFET paper (621) has heaviest formalism but all 14 equations are necessary for physics-based device modeling. Final: 0 CRITICAL, 0 LIMITED, 4 ADEQUATE, 2 STRONG.
